symptoms.symptom_name: Severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, refractory to maximum-dose PPI

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PPI-refractory severe ulcer pain is a hallmark of gastric-acid hypersecretion from a gastrinoma.
A simple ulcer should respond to maximum-dose PPI; failure argues against it as the whole story.
Severe unremitting ulcer pain flags impending perforation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe colicky left flank pain radiating to the groin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic loin-to-groin colicky pain in a recurrent stone-former.
Recurrent calcium stones in a MEN context point to hypercalcaemia from hyperparathyroidism.
Acid hypersecretion inactivates pancreatic enzymes and injures mucosa, producing secretory diarrhoea.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss over six months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional weight loss supports a systemic neoplastic syndrome over a benign local ulcer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strong multi-generational family history of endocrine tumours (sibling pituitary tumour; father died of ruptured gastric ulcer)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: MEN1 is autosomal dominant; a multi-generational endocrine-tumour history strongly raises the prior probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Episodic severe headache with profuse diaphoresis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paroxysmal headache, sweating and hypertension form the classic phaeochromocytoma triad; must be excluded before procedures.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent NSAID (ibuprofen) use with an active ulcer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: NSAIDs are a leading, reversible driver of ulcer complications; cessation is an immediate safety lever.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mucosal neuromas on lips and tip of tongue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucosal neuromas are a recognised MEN phenotype feature (classically MEN2B); a patient may notice lumps but professional identification requires examination. Subtype discrepancy flagged in node 10.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bitemporal visual field defect on confrontation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: pituitary adenoma with optic chiasm compression
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A bitemporal field defect indicates chiasmal compression from a pituitary mass — cannot be reliably assessed via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe epigastric tenderness to deep palpation without rebound/rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked tenderness without peritonism suggests severe ulceration but not yet frank perforation; a physical finding unavailable via telehealth.
CVA tenderness supports upper-urinary-tract origin; requires clinician palpation.
Clinician-measured vitals; elevated BP/HR could reflect catecholamine excess or severe pain. Sealed measurement, not patient-obtainable here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated serum gastrin, calcium and PTH (anticipated on tertiary work-up)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Fasting serum gastrin, corrected calcium and PTH
investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated gastrin (gastrinoma) and elevated calcium with inappropriately high PTH (primary hyperparathyroidism)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The hormonal drivers are missed and treatment remains ineffective.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biochemical hormone excess plus imaging/genetics confirms the syndrome — a hospital investigation never available in real-time telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Suspected MEN1 with gastrinoma and primary hyperparathyroidism
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: PPI-refractory ulcer + secretory diarrhoea + recurrent calcium stones + endocrine-tumour family history + mucosal/pituitary features together point to a multi-gland neoplastic syndrome, not isolated disease.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Impending ulcer perforation/haemorrhage and/or obstructed infected renal tract
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The time-critical threats that mandate emergency escalation regardless of the syndrome label.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital assessment with CT abdomen/pelvis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude perforation and characterise the obstructing renal calculus; the AI Doctor arranges this via escalation, it does not order imaging directly.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting serum gastrin, corrected calcium and PTH
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the hypersecretory and hypercalcaemic drivers of the presentation.
Exclude phaeochromocytoma given episodic headache, diaphoresis and hypertension before any procedure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: MEN1 genetic testing and first-degree-relative screening
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Establishes the syndrome and enables family cascade screening — a tertiary/outpatient step.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical H. pylori eradication as the primary explanation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Chasing H. pylori as the answer would anchor on a simple ulcer and delay recognition of the hypersecretory syndrome.
